name: Osprey
ecosystem: Water. Rivers, lakes, estuaries, coastal waters. Most water-dependent raptor in PNW.
season: Summer migratory. Arrives March/April, departs August/September. Winters South America.
conservation: Least Concern (IUCN). ~460,000 global population. DDT-impacted, now recovered.

vocalization.description: Loud, sharp series: chewk-chewk-chewk (also kyew-kyew-kyew or pee-pee-pee). Penetrating, carries over open water.
vocalization.function: Nest defense, territorial advertisement, mate calling, food solicitation, alarm
vocalization.notable: Vocally active species. Calls readily. Designed to carry over open water acoustic environment.

anatomical_adaptations: Reversible outer toe (zygodactyl) -- 2 toes per side on fish
Oily plumage -- rapid water shedding after plunge
Head-forward fish carry instinct -- aerodynamic drag reduction

connections.description: Mad Season / Above (E=6). Zone between water and sky. Staley's aquatic lyrics. Osprey dives into water from sky. Above inhabits the threshold. Most resonant pair in raptor era.
Bald Eagle steals fish from Osprey (kleptoparasitism). Franklin's complaint at 221. The arc closes at 224 with the victim's entry.
After 3 Accipitridae (221-223), FIRST PANDIONIDAE at 224. Open country Golden Eagle to water-surface Osprey.
